summary: Regression test post evictMoveBuffer local-resync fix. Verified move-processing/detection flow via window.__trainer/window.__cube debug hooks. All 4 required scenarios pass with zero console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: Note (informational, not blocking): Real BLE evictMoveBuffer resync path cannot be exercised in automation without a physical GAN cube; code inspection matches described fix (moveBuffer cleared, lastSerial=serial, no conn.disconnect).
seed_data_creation: None. Used window.__cube.SOLVED + applyMove to simulate facelet updates.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend debug hooks window.__trainer {getState, getTarget, getSuccess, solveCurrent, feedFacelets, markSolved} and window.__cube {SOLVED, applyMove, applyAlg, scramble} are reliable for simulating BLE facelet events. testids used: recognition-timer (data-timer-state waiting|running), letter-pair-display. First feedFacelets(SOLVED) sets reference; subsequent feeds change getState relative to it.

test_results_detail.test1_move_detection: PASS — feedFacelets(SOLVED) => state==SOLVED, timer=waiting. feedFacelets(applyMove(SOLVED,'R')) => state changed, timer=running.
test_results_detail.test2_solve_advances: PASS — solveCurrent() incremented solved counter to 1, letter pair changed 'UR' -> 'KI', timer running.
test_results_detail.test3_burst_15_moves: PASS — 15 random face turns (U R F D L B) applied and fed; getState() matches simulated state, no uncaught errors, no error overlay, 0 console errors, 0 warnings.
test_results_detail.test4_keyboard_moves: PASS — pressing r/u/f changed state relative to SOLVED reference and timer moved to running with no console errors.

rca of the issue: N/A — regression pass; BLE-layer fix (evictMoveBuffer local resync replacing conn.disconnect) cannot be reproduced without physical hardware.
